jokes.text: What happened to your Whitney Houston cutout? She fell apart in the shower.
jokes.setup: Asking about missing Whitney Houston cutout
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was taken into the shower and destroyed
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual of taking a cardboard cutout into the shower, delivered with perfect deadpan economy

jokes.text: My building's being evacuated due to bubonic plague.
jokes.setup: Jazz explaining why he needs to stay
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of medieval disease in modern LA
jokes.explanation: Jazz's tendency toward absurd explanations, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Jazz, you're about as welcome here as Mike Tyson at a beauty pageant.
jokes.setup: Will rejecting Jazz's request to stay
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture comparison highlighting unwelcomeness
jokes.explanation: Solid simile structure with timely reference, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Man, just throw us in the gutter. Looks like rain. You got any Scotchgard for Bill?
jokes.setup: Jazz being dramatic about rejection
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about weather-proofing his ventriloquist dummy
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation from melodrama to practical weather concerns for a dummy

jokes.text: What would Bill do in that situation? Usually Pudding Pops.
jokes.setup: Jazz claiming Bill is his role model for decision-making
jokes.punchline: Revealing the dummy's only advice is a snack food
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of the 'wise mentor' trope with absurd specificity and economy

jokes.text: Kuwaiti was my last hope. And he suspects me of borrowing his whitewalls. I did, but he ain't got no right to make that assumption.
jokes.setup: Jazz explaining why his friend Kuwaiti won't help
jokes.punchline: Admitting guilt while claiming moral high ground
jokes.explanation: Jazz's twisted logic perfectly in character - guilty but indignant about being suspected

jokes.text: Shoot, right now, I ain't even got a friend in Jesus.
jokes.setup: Jazz expressing his desperate situation
jokes.punchline: Twist on the phrase 'friend in Jesus' suggesting even divine rejection
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on religious expression fits Jazz's dramatic tendencies

jokes.text: I will, however, require complete silence during The Young and the Restless.
jokes.setup: Jazz agreeing to be quiet as a houseguest
jokes.punchline: Making exception for soap opera viewing
jokes.explanation: Character detail that contrasts Jazz's street persona with soap opera obsession

jokes.text: Geoffrey, it sounds like you're getting a cold. Not at all, madam. It was barely a stipple.
jokes.setup: Concern about Geoffrey's health
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's pretentious word choice while clearly sick
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's characteristic pompous denial while obviously ill

jokes.text: As you bish, madam.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey trying to maintain dignity while sick
jokes.punchline: Nasal congestion affecting his formal speech
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective physical comedy through speech impediment

jokes.text: Isn't free parking guaranteed by the Constitution?
jokes.setup: Carlton's reaction to Hilary's traffic violations
jokes.punchline: Earnestly suggesting constitutional right to free parking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton naivety - applying lofty constitutional principles to mundane parking

jokes.text: Yeah, that's what Desert Storm was all about.
jokes.setup: Carlton's constitutional parking claim
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically connecting parking rights to Gulf War
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic comeback with timely Gulf War reference

jokes.text: Can't a brother get a pedicure?
jokes.setup: Will knowing the location of South Side clinic
jokes.punchline: Revealing he knows it from going to nearby nail salon
jokes.explanation: Will's street credibility undercut by admission of beauty treatments

jokes.text: Hey, after the last time, I just bought a car cover.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking about his missing bathrobe
jokes.punchline: Will referencing previous incident by buying car cover for protection
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that implies embarrassing previous incident without explaining it

jokes.text: Sure, blame the black guy.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking about missing cassette player
jokes.punchline: Will pointing out automatic suspicion falling on him
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: That's my cassette player. That's my bathrobe. That's my girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Family discovers Jazz wearing/using their stolen items
jokes.punchline: Ashley claims the girl with Jazz as 'my girlfriend'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with unexpected punchline. Ashley claiming Jazz's girl as her girlfriend is a great surprise.

jokes.text: But enough about Marion Barry. Can Jazz stay here?
jokes.setup: Will describing someone down on their luck who lost everything
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was talking about the scandal-ridden DC mayor, not Jazz
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with timely political reference, perfect setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Geoffrey bathes. I can learn.
jokes.setup: Jazz offering to do whatever Geoffrey does
jokes.punchline: Admitting he doesn't currently bathe but is willing to learn
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing, reveals Jazz's hygiene issues with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: If you need me, I'll be asleep under your car. But just honk before you throw it in reverse.
jokes.setup: Jazz accepting rejection and leaving
jokes.punchline: Practical safety concern about being run over while homeless
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about homelessness with practical twist, escalates guilt trip effectively

jokes.text: I sleep naked with the window open. I hope that don't bother you. No, that's cool with me. You're sleeping in Carlton's room.
jokes.setup: Jazz warning Will about his sleeping habits
jokes.punchline: Will redirecting the problem to Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience expects Will to be bothered, but he deflects to Carlton

jokes.text: No, that's cool with me. You're sleeping in Carlton's room.
jokes.setup: Jazz shares uncomfortable details about his sleeping habits
jokes.punchline: Will redirects the discomfort to Carlton
jokes.explanation: Great Will move - deflecting Jazz's weirdness onto Carlton. Good character dynamics.

jokes.text: Get the heck out of here.
jokes.setup: Hilary seeing sick people at the clinic
jokes.punchline: Her privileged shock at encountering actual illness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary reaction - sheltered rich girl encountering real world

jokes.text: That cute little tenderoni there got my pressure so high, I'm about to blow a gasket.
jokes.setup: Old man getting blood pressure checked
jokes.punchline: Sexual attraction causing literal high blood pressure
jokes.explanation: Standard dirty old man joke with automotive metaphor

jokes.text: Hey there, Herman. No, it's Hilary. But you can call me Herman if you want to.
jokes.setup: Patient mishearing Hilary's name
jokes.punchline: Hilary being overly accommodating about the mistake
jokes.explanation: Hilary's people-pleasing nature taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Well, not exactly, but I've seen every single episode of St. Elsewhere.
jokes.setup: Asked about medical experience
jokes.punchline: Claiming TV medical drama as qualification
jokes.explanation: Predictable joke about TV medical shows, but fits Hilary's character

jokes.text: That's not gonna happen here.
jokes.setup: Hilary referencing romantic TV medical drama episodes
jokes.punchline: Nurse's blunt dismissal of romantic expectations
jokes.explanation: Good straight-woman response with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: It's sort of a shooting pain. It shoots from one ankle to the other.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking which ankle is injured
jokes.punchline: Hilary describing impossible traveling ankle pain
jokes.explanation: Hilary's medical ignorance creating impossible symptom description

jokes.text: Thank you for marrying... Carrying her.
jokes.setup: Vivian trying to thank the doctor
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip revealing her hopes for Hilary
jokes.explanation: Standard Freudian slip joke revealing mother's matchmaking desires

jokes.text: Do you come in a smaller size?
jokes.setup: Ashley meeting the tall, handsome doctor
jokes.punchline: Precocious kid wanting a child-sized version
jokes.explanation: Ashley's precocious attraction to older men delivered with perfect innocence

jokes.text: Well, if you ever gave me any thigh, maybe I'd know the difference.
jokes.setup: Jazz touching Hilary's ankle, Philip objecting to touching thigh
jokes.punchline: Philip's sexual frustration revealed through ankle/thigh confusion
jokes.explanation: Uncle Phil's rare moment of sexual frustration humor

jokes.text: Jazz. I've had it with you, man! I'm about to... thank you for starching my lucky drawers.
jokes.setup: Will appearing angry at Jazz
jokes.punchline: Switching to gratitude for underwear care
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection with absurd underwear care appreciation

jokes.text: Not only that, they're bulletproof.
jokes.setup: Will praising the starch job on his underwear
jokes.punchline: Claiming excessive starch made them bulletproof
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the starch joke with street-relevant bulletproof twist

jokes.text: Malcolm's one of my heroes, too. I idolize Malcolm. He's the only reason I watch The Cosby Show.
jokes.setup: Meeting Dr. Alec, thinking of Malcolm X
jokes.punchline: Confusing Malcolm X with Malcolm-Jamal Warner from Cosby Show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz moment - trying to sound deep but revealing pop culture confusion

jokes.text: After I spent all morning frying the salami.
jokes.setup: Jazz noticing garlic smell everywhere
jokes.punchline: Revealing he fried salami for lunch, explaining the smell
jokes.explanation: Jazz's weird cooking choices explaining mysterious garlic smell

jokes.text: I wasn't always, but it was the only way I could get a scholarship.
jokes.setup: Will commenting that Alec is black
jokes.punchline: Doctor claiming he changed race for educational benefits
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of affirmative action stereotypes with absurd racial change claim

jokes.text: Before I stuck it in the machine, I washed my hands.
jokes.setup: Will complains his shirt says 'Hand wash' and Jazz ruined it
jokes.punchline: Jazz literally interpreted 'hand wash' as washing hands before machine washing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literalist humor that's perfectly Jazz. The logic is absurd but internally consistent, showing his well-meaning incompetence.

jokes.text: Before I stuck it in the machine, I washed my hands.
jokes.setup: Will angry about Jazz ruining his hand-wash-only clothes
jokes.punchline: Jazz's literal interpretation of 'hand wash' instruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz logic - following instructions completely literally while missing the point

jokes.text: Now, baby, why don't you sit down and never get up?
jokes.setup: Vivian being polite to Jazz as a guest
jokes.punchline: Backhanded hospitality suggesting permanent immobility
jokes.explanation: Perfect passive-aggressive hospitality with thinly veiled hostility

jokes.text: which is easier for some than others.
jokes.setup: Jazz claiming he pulls his own weight
jokes.punchline: Subtle dig at Uncle Phil's size
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke delivered with Jazz's characteristic subtlety

jokes.text: Thank God, 'cause I thought you were singing.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey explaining he was regurgitating
jokes.punchline: Jazz claiming the vomiting sounds better than Geoffrey's singing
jokes.explanation: Good insult comedy comparing vomiting favorably to singing

jokes.text: There ain't enough for him.
jokes.setup: Hilary inviting Alec to stay for dinner
jokes.punchline: Jazz suggesting Uncle Phil will eat too much
jokes.explanation: Another standard Jazz fat joke about Uncle Phil's eating habits

jokes.text: She sure was. I'm sorry I lost her number.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil praising the cherries jubilee dessert
jokes.punchline: Doctor pretending to think it was a woman
jokes.explanation: Standard wordplay treating dessert name as woman's name

jokes.text: I suppose everybody's too full to eat the Pop-Tarts I made for dessert.
jokes.setup: After elaborate cherries jubilee dessert
jokes.punchline: Jazz offering processed toaster pastries as dessert
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz moment - following gourmet cooking with junk food

jokes.text: Just like black folk. Eat, then they got to go to sleep.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil going upstairs to relax after dinner
jokes.punchline: Jazz making racial stereotype comment
jokes.explanation: Tired racial stereotype humor that relies on cliché

jokes.text: If he don't know whether or not somebody's alive, he's in the wrong damn business.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying Alec doesn't know she's alive
jokes.punchline: Jazz taking the metaphor literally regarding medical practice
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jazz logic - literal interpretation of romantic metaphor applied to medical competence

jokes.text: If you want me to leave the room, just ask.
jokes.setup: Will obviously trying to get Jazz to leave
jokes.punchline: Jazz calling out the transparent attempt
jokes.explanation: Jazz showing rare awareness of social dynamics

jokes.text: Hey, baby. I'm Jazz, a close, personal friend of Bill Cosby. Did I mention you're the only woman I ever loved?
jokes.setup: Jazz hitting on a woman at the door
jokes.punchline: Rapid escalation from celebrity name-drop to love declaration
jokes.explanation: Classic Jazz pickup approach with celebrity association and instant commitment

jokes.text: Don't mention that to my fiancée, Hilary. Jazz, that is Hilary.
jokes.setup: Jazz hitting on woman at door claiming he has fiancée
jokes.punchline: Revealing the woman IS his supposed fiancée Hilary
jokes.explanation: Good mistaken identity payoff with Jazz's typical lack of awareness

jokes.text: that's a trash compactor! When Uncle Phil sees that it's gonna be a Jazz compactor!
jokes.setup: Jazz destroyed Uncle Phil's briefcase in what he thought was a drawer
jokes.punchline: Will's pun about Jazz becoming the next thing to be compacted
jokes.explanation: Solid pun with threat escalation, plays on Fresh Prince's signature 'thrown out' gag

jokes.text: We sound like an old married couple. So now I'm old?
jokes.setup: Will observes they're bickering like a married couple
jokes.punchline: Jazz takes offense at 'old' rather than 'married couple'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where Jazz focuses on wrong part of the comparison. Shows his vanity and selective listening.

jokes.text: Course you could be cross-eyed with a rash and still take my cash.
jokes.setup: Herman complimenting Hilary's new look
jokes.punchline: Crude expression of attraction regardless of flaws
jokes.explanation: Standard dirty old man humor with rhyming structure

jokes.text: I ain't got that much time left. I'll be back in three.
jokes.setup: Told to come back in 6-8 hours for medication
jokes.punchline: Elderly patient joking about mortality to justify shorter wait
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about aging and mortality with practical application

jokes.text: Mandela was freed two years ago. And I, for one, want to make sure he stays that way.
jokes.setup: Hilary claiming she was arrested at a Free Mandela rally
jokes.punchline: Her not knowing Mandela was already freed, doubling down on ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - political ignorance covered by confident-sounding nonsense

jokes.text: Is 7:01 okay?
jokes.setup: Alec saying party starts at 7:00, Hilary might be late
jokes.punchline: Suggesting arrival one minute late as compromise
jokes.explanation: Hilary's literal-mindedness and eagerness perfectly captured in one line

jokes.text: Well, no. But I did talk several of them into shaving under their arms.
jokes.setup: Asked if she was a member of women's group in college
jokes.punchline: Her contribution was enforcing beauty standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Hilary's complete misunderstanding of feminism

jokes.text: Get the heck out of here, I'm one of them, too. Yeah, well, down at the hospital, they refer to me as Brother Doogie.
jokes.setup: Will claiming to be a doctor too
jokes.punchline: Alec's reference to Doogie Howser with racial twist
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with racial element, fits the early 90s setting

jokes.text: Who's up for meatloaf?
jokes.setup: Intense discussion about women's issues and oppression
jokes.punchline: Hilary interrupting with domestic food offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing joke - interrupting feminist discourse with traditional domestic role

jokes.text: Next time I donate an organ, I'm just gonna let someone else perform the surgery.
jokes.setup: Hilary explaining why she's tired
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning performing surgery on herself
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Hilary logic - self-performed organ donation surgery delivered casually

jokes.text: Are you teaching her to read?
jokes.setup: Learning Danielle and Alec aren't related
jokes.punchline: Hilary's condescending assumption about their relationship
jokes.explanation: Hilary's elitist assumptions about why an educated man would spend time with a woman

jokes.text: And I lied. I really did think Eldridge Cleaver was a big chopping knife.
jokes.setup: Hilary's final outburst after being rejected
jokes.punchline: Admitting she confused civil rights leader with kitchen utensil
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - intellectual pretension completely deflated by absurd confession

jokes.text: Jazz, would you prefer a Muslim or a Viking burial?
jokes.setup: Jazz making inappropriate comments about marrying Hilary
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil offering burial options as death threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent Uncle Phil threat humor with unexpected cultural sensitivity in murder planning
